data.title: Why Samsung Stock Hit a New Record High Today
data.description:
Samsung Electronics' (SSNLF) Korea-listed shares hit a new record high on Thursday. The move followed a local media report that the company is negotiating a price for its newest AI memory chip that could be up to 30% higher than the prior version. Shares rose as much as 5.4% on the Korea Exchange when trading resumed after a three-day holiday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Samsung Electronics stock closed 54% at $65.21 on Wednesday as investors reacted to rising demand for AI hardware.

Higher HBM Prices Signal Strong AI Demand

According to Chosun Ilbo, Samsung plans to price its next-generation high-bandwidth memory, HBM4, at about $700 per unit. This type of memory works together with AI chips inside data-center servers. Because these chips run large AI models and heavy tasks, higher prices suggest demand is still stronger than supply.

For investors, this shows large cloud companies are still expanding AI capacity, supporting both memory makers and AI chip suppliers.

Samsung Starts HBM4 Deliveries

Last week, Samsung said it began mass production of HBM4 and has already shipped products to customers. The move could deepen ties with major AI chip designers such as Nvidia (NVDA), which relies on fast memory to run its GPUs.

The company also noted better performance. HBM4 reaches speeds of about 11.7 gigabits per second, around 22% faster than the earlier HBM3E version. Faster memory helps AI servers run more efficiently and handle bigger workloads.

What's Happening with Samsung Stock? 

Samsung's Korea-listed stock has surged this year on the back of massive chip demand fueled by the AI boom. The shares are up nearly 130%, while the OTC-listed shares have climbed more than 40% over the same period.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Meta CEO Says "We Don't Do That Anymore" as Teen Addiction Trial Puts META Stock Under Scrutiny
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is in court this week as a major trial tests claims that social media can harm young users. Mark Zuckerberg took the stand in Los Angeles and faced hard questions about how Facebook and Instagram were built to hold attention. For years, critics have argued that features like infinite scroll and autoplay make it hard for teens to log off. Zuckerberg said Meta no longer sets goals around time spent, adding, "We used to give teams goals on time spent, and we don't do that anymore."

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of the case is a young woman known as K.G.M., who says she began using Instagram at age 10 and later struggled with anxiety, depression, and addiction. In fact, Meta and Alphabet (GOOGL) are facing the first of thousands of similar lawsuits. The plaintiffs argue that the focus is not on user posts but on how platforms are designed. That approach may help them get around legal shields that often protect tech firms.

Meanwhile, META shares rose 0.61% on Wednesday, closing at $643.22.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Microsoft Signals Commercial Quantum Breakthrough: Stock Implications for MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, and IONQ
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is making a clear statement that is starting to matter for the broader AI market and investors. The company's corporate vice president of Quantum, Zulfi Alam, told CNBC, "By the end of the decade, we are confident that we will have machines in data centers that have commercial value." He also added that by 2029, these systems will be doing work "that classical machines cannot do."

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, Microsoft is telling the market that quantum is moving closer to real use, not just lab tests. For MSFT, the stock angle is not near-term sales, but long-term platform value. If quantum becomes part of the data center stack, Microsoft could be in a strong spot as both a cloud leader and a key tool provider.

At the same time, the company also notes that quantum will not replace today's systems. Alam said, "A quantum machine is not a standalone entity. It's a hybrid tool." That means the upside is tied to added demand, not a full shift away from current AI and cloud spend.

Meanwhile, MSFT shares moved up slightly on Wednesday, closing at $399.60.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Etsy Stock Pops on Sale of Secondhand Fashion Platform Depop to eBay
data.description:
Etsy (ETSY) stock jumped 16.4% in Wednesday's extended trading session after the online marketplace announced an agreement to sell its secondhand online clothing platform Depop to e-commerce retailer eBay (EBAY) for $1.2 billion in cash. The transaction is expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2026. The news comes ahead of Etsy's fourth-quarter earnings release before the market opens on Thursday, February 19.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wall Street expects Etsy to report an 18.4% year-over-year decline in its earnings per share (EPS) to $0.84 for Q4 2025.

Meanwhile, eBay stock rose 6% in Wednesday's after-hours trading on news of the Depop deal and the company's better-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings.

Etsy Offloads Depop to eBay  

Etsy bought Depop in 2021 for about $1.62 billion, as the company believed that the move would give its platform access to younger customers interested in buying and selling their used clothes, accessories, and other items. Aside from Depop, Etsy acquired many niche online platforms as part of its "house of brands" strategy to compete with larger rivals like Amazon (AMZN).

However, Etsy offloaded some of these marketplaces, including Elo7 and Reverb, as it struggled to thrive after the pandemic-led e-commerce boom faded. Aside from Amazon, the company faces stiff competition from Shopify (SHOP) and low-cost players such as Temu and TikTok Shop. Regarding the Depop sale, Etsy CEO Kruti Patel Goyal said that the deal would allow the company to focus exclusively on its Etsy marketplace.

Meanwhile, eBay expects Depop to advance its rapidly growing Focus Categories and help it gain access to a younger demographic in the expanding "recommerce landscape."

Is ETSY Stock a Buy Right Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on Etsy stock based on 11 Holds, two Buys, and one Sell recommendation. The average ETSY stock price target of $61.58 indicates about 40% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: UK's Starmer Vows to "Crack Down on the Addictive Elements of Social Media," Challenging Meta and YouTube
data.description:
Europe and Asia are moving fast toward new limits on teen social media use, and that could matter for big platforms like Meta (META), Snap Inc. (SNAP), and Alphabet (GOOGL). There's a sweeping trend as more countries look to block younger users from apps built around endless-scroll feeds. As a result, the debate is no longer just about parenting and health. It is also becoming a real business issue for the tech sector.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of this push is the idea that social apps may be linked to rising stress and harm among teens. For example, Australia has already moved to block access for users under 16. Meanwhile, France has passed a plan to ban social media for users under 15, and Spain is looking at a limit of under 16. In addition, the UK is starting a public review of similar rules next month.

Policy leaders are also focusing on how these apps keep users engaged. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer wrote that the goal is to "crack down on the addictive elements of social media" and stop "the never-ending scrolling." French President Emmanuel Macron also warned that AI-driven feeds can guide users in ways they do not fully see.

Tech firms, however, say these bans may not work as intended. Snap's Jean Gonié said, "Bans will only push kids toward smaller apps with fewer safety protections." YouTube has also argued that broad limits may not lead to safer use for teens.

The Business Stakes for Big Platforms

From a financial standpoint, the key question is how these rules may shape growth over time. Most large platforms already limit targeted ads for minors, so the near-term hit may be small. However, the bigger risk is the long-term user pipeline. If teens cannot join early, fewer may stay loyal as adults.

In the end, stricter age limits could raise costs for compliance, reduce future user growth, and add more legal pressure. That could weigh on both the top line through slower audience expansion and the bottom line through higher policy and safety spending. Over time, that makes this global trend an issue that markets may need to watch closely.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to assemble all notable publicly traded social media stocks to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/19/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were higher ahead of Thursday's session after the major indexes closed in positive territory. Traders are now looking ahead to Walmart's (WMT) quarterly results, due Thursday morning, which could give clues about consumer spending. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.26%, 0.09%, and 0.18%, respectively, at 2:25 a.m. EST on February 19.

During Wednesday's regular session, the S&P 500, the Nasdaq, and the Dow Jones climbed 0.6%, 0.8%, and 0.3%, respectively. Tech leaders in the "Magnificent Seven," along with financial and energy stocks, helped lift the market.

Geopolitical tensions on Wednesday pushed oil prices up more than 4% after Vice President JD Vance said Iran failed to meet key U.S. demands in this week's nuclear talks. He added that President Donald Trump could still use military force if diplomacy does not stop Iran's nuclear program.

Investors also inflation-remains-above-2-minutes-show">reviewed minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting, which showed officials were divided on the interest-rate outlook.

Walmart's earnings on Thursday will be closely watched, given the retailer's strong performance in 2026 so far and its recent move into the $1 trillion market cap club. Also, traders are awaiting weekly jobless claims and pending home sales data due tomorrow.

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Key Insider Buys $1.98M in Stock — Bullish Signal for Investors?
data.description:
A fresh insider purchase is turning heads at Microsoft Corporation (MSFT). Company director John W. Stanton disclosed a $1.98 million stock buy, adding 5,000 shares to his position for $397.35 per share. Insider transactions often draw attention from retail investors because they can signal confidence in a company's outlook. Following the latest transaction, Stanton directly owns 83,905 shares of MSFT. In addition, he indirectly holds 3,622 shares through a family trust. Year-to-date, MSFT stock is down by over 17%.

Insider Sentiment for MSFT Stock Is Bearish

Overall, TipRanks' Insider Trading Activity Tool indicates that insider confidence in MSFT stock is currently Negative, suggesting a cautious tone behind the scenes. Over the past three months, corporate insiders have executed informative sell transactions totaling approximately $4.5 million worth of shares. While the tech giant continues to benefit from AI momentum and strong cloud demand, insider transactions in recent months have tilted more toward selling than buying — a pattern some investors view as a potentially bearish signal.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Is HIMS Stock a Buy Ahead of Earnings? Options Traders Brace for 17% Price Swing
data.description:
Hims & Hers (HIMS) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after market close on February 23. Analysts expect earnings to decline but sales to rise modestly in the December quarter

HIMS stock faces backlash for selling copycat weight-loss drugs on its pharmacy platform, along with U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) scrutiny and a lawsuit from Wegovy maker Novo Nordisk (NVO). Investors seek further clarity on these issues during the earnings call. Any negative signals could drive shares lower.

Hims & Hers is a telehealth company, offering prescription medications, wellness products, and online consultations. Year-to-date, HIMS shares have plunged more than 51% due to uncertainty around its GLP-1 drug offerings.

Expectations from Hims & Hers

The Street expects Hims & Hers to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.04, much lower than the prior year's figure of $0.11. Sales are forecast to jump 28.7% year-over-year to $619.20 million. It is worth noting that the company has beaten earnings estimates in six of the past eight quarters.

Moreover, according to TipRanks' Options Traders Tool, options traders expect about a 16.92% move in either direction for HIMS stock in reaction to Q4 FY25 results. This implied move is significantly higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 5.04% over the past four quarters.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: All Eyes on Nvidia: Bulls and Bears Weigh In Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25, with expectations running high. The stock has climbed about 35% over the past year, driven by strong demand for its AI data-center chips. Ahead of earnings, investors are divided on what comes next. Some expect another strong outlook tied to the Blackwell ramp, while others remain cautious about margins, China export restrictions, supply constraints, and rising competition. Here's a closer look at what bulls and bears are saying before the release.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Driving the Bullish Case Into Earnings?

1. AI Spending from Cloud Giants Remains Strong: Major cloud companies such as Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL) continue to raise AI infrastructure spending. In their latest earnings calls, each company highlighted plans to increase capital spending to build AI data centers and capacity. Bulls believe Nvidia remains the main supplier of AI accelerators, supporting continued data-center revenue growth.

2. Meta Partnership Adds Visibility: The recently expanded multiyear AI infrastructure partnership with Meta (META) signals sustained demand for large AI clusters. Bulls view this as evidence that hyperscaler investment in AI is not slowing. In a new note, Stifel kept a Buy rating and said the deal shows Meta's growing reliance on Nvidia's full-stack AI systems, including GPUs and networking.

3. Blackwell Ramp Could Drive the Next Leg Higher: Investors expect shipments of the new Blackwell GPUs to begin scaling this year. Bulls say the new platform should bring another upgrade cycle for customers and could extend Nvidia's growth into 2026.

4. Wall Street Analysts Remain Positive: Wall Street continues to rate the stock a Strong Buy, pointing to Nvidia's dominant position in AI computing and its software ecosystem advantage. Strong forward guidance, rather than the reported quarter itself, is seen as the key upside catalyst. UBS recently raised his price target to $245 and said supply-chain checks remain healthy, while Goldman expects another revenue and earnings beat driven by hyperscaler spending.

Why Some See Limited Upside

1. China Restrictions Could Affect Guidance: Nvidia's advanced GPUs remain subject to U.S. export controls to China, a market that previously contributed meaningful data-center revenue. Bears worry the company may exclude China sales from forward guidance, which could lower growth expectations even if the quarterly results are strong.

2. Investors are closely watching gross margins: Rising high-bandwidth memory (HBM) costs, a critical component in AI accelerators, along with potential export-related expenses could push margins below the mid-70% range Wall Street expects. A margin decline would likely weigh on the stock.

3. Rising Competition from Cloud Customers: Major hyperscalers are increasingly developing their own AI chips and optimizing workloads. While Nvidia still dominates AI training, bears argue growing in-house solutions at large cloud companies could gradually limit future pricing power and growth.

4. High Expectations Leave Little Room for Error: After a massive multi-year rally, expectations are extremely high heading into earnings. Bears believe the stock may react more to forward guidance than reported results, meaning even a modestly cautious outlook could trigger volatility.

Key Numbers to Watch in Q4 Earnings

According to Wall Street estimates, Nvidia is expected to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are expected to jump 68% year-over-year to $65.67 billion.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
